title: Apparatus and method for inverting items
abstract: An apparatus to serially invert items. The configured to invert items, either one or multiple at one time. There is preferably an entry conveyer configured to support and convey items towards the apparatus. The apparatus comprises a tilting assembly. The tilting assembly is configured to tilt, to invert said items. Once inverted the items are egressed from the tilting assembly to an exit conveyer. The item may be a stack of trays carrying items such as fruit, once the stack is subsequently inverted, a case or box may be laid over the inverted stack.
claims: Claims 1. An apparatus to invert items serially, wherein the apparatus defines a substrate onto which the items are or are to be serially presented, a part of such substrate (whether the same part or not in each instance) to co-act with a tilting assembly, and a surface ('coacting surface') to act as a support of an item inverted between the coacting surface and the part of the substrate, and a tilting assembly configured to rotate the substrate and coacting surface on a tilt axis aligned with, or parallel to, the axis of serial presentation, wherein the tilting assembly is configured to allow a or the part of the substrate and a or part of the coacting surface to swap roles for a subsequent item to be inverted. 2. An apparatus as claimed in claim 1, such items being cuboid or rectanguloid cases (whether open or closed) or cuboid or rectanguloid assemblages of trays of or for such cases, or a stack of individual items to be subsequently contained within a case once inverted. 3. An apparatus as claimed in claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the tilting assembly alternates its rotation clockwise to anticlockwise, or vice versa, for the proximate items or prior to presentation of a subsequent item. 4. An apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein either or both of the substrate and coacting surface are part of a conveyor, the conveyor configured to facilitate conveying the item between the coacting surface and the substrate. 5. An apparatus as claimed in claim 4, wherein the conveyor is either driven or passive. 6. An apparatus as claimed in claim 4 or 5, wherein the conveyor comprises a series of rollers. 7. An apparatus as claimed in claim 4 or 5, wherein conveyor is a ball transfer conveyor or wheel conveyor. 8. An apparatus as claimed in claim 4 or 5, wherein the conveyor comprises a belt about the series of rollers. 9. An apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the substrate and coacting surface are substantially parallel to each other. 10. An apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the substrate is horizontal. 11. An apparatus as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the substrate, and associated coacting surface, is sloped downward in the direction of serial presentation. 12. An apparatus as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the substrate, and associated coacting surface, is sloped upwards in the direction of serial presentation. 13. An apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the tilting assembly is supported by rollers to facilitate the rotation of the coacting surface and substrate. 14. An apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the apparatus comprises a base, and the tilting apparatus is at least partially supported on the base by rollers. 15. An apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the tilting assembly is rotationally driven by a belt drive. 16. An apparatus as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein the tilting assembly is rotationally driven by the rollers. 17. An apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the tilting assembly rotates more than 100Â° on the rotation axis for substantial inversion of each item. 18. An apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the tilting assembly rotates between 165Â° and 180Â° on the rotation axis for inversion of each item. 19. An apparatus as claimed in claim 18, wherein the tilting assembly rotates 180Â° on the rotation axis for inversion of each item. 20. An apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the tilting assembly comprises wheels, that house the substrate and coacting surface within their periphery. 21. An apparatus as claimed in claim 20, wherein the tilting assembly comprises two wheels, and one wheel is driven. 22. An apparatus as claimed in claim 21, wherein the substrate and coacting surface are located symmetrically and centrally within the wheels. 23. An apparatus as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the apparatus comprises a conveyor to serially present items to the substrate. 24. A method of inverting items, the method comprising or including the steps of sequentially positioning by item movement and/or apparatus movement each item between a substrate surface and a coacting surface and synchronously rotating both surfaces and the item on an axis parallel to the axis or axes of the item and/or apparatus movement. 25. An apparatus as claimed in claim 24, wherein the both surfaces and the item are rotated from one selected of; over 160 degrees, over 170 degrees, 180 degrees, and over 180 degrees. 26. An apparatus as claimed in claim 24 or 25, wherein the surfaces alternate in their role for the subsequent item. 27. An apparatus as claimed in any one of claims 24 to 26, wherein the surfaces rotate clockwise for an inversion of a first item(s) and subsequently rotate counter clockwise for rotation of a subsequent item, or alternately, the surfaces rotate counter clockwise to be ready to receive the subsequent item to be inverted clockwise like the first item. 28. An apparatus as claimed in any one of claims 24 to 27, wherein each item is a cuboid or rectanguloid case (open or closed) or a cuboid or rectanguloid assemblage of trays, each for such a case; or a stack of individual items to be subsequently contained, within a case, once inverted.
